cards.back: A paging strategy in which a page is brought into memory only when it is needed by a process.
A technique used to allow multiple processes to share the same page in memory by creating a copy of the page when it is first modified.
There are several algorithms used to replace pages in the main memory, such as First-In-First-Out (FIFO), Least Recently Used (LRU), and Optimal (Opt), to minimize page faults.
A small cache of page table entries used to speed up the process of translating virtual addresses to physical addresses.
An exception that occurs when a process accesses a page that is not in memory, requiring the operating system to page it in.
